# Glossary

- [Person-centred care](person-centred-care.md) — Care planned and delivered in partnership with the person, respecting their needs, preferences, values and right to be involved in decisions.
- [Evidence-based practice](evidence-based-practice.md) — Practising in line with the best available evidence, keeping knowledge and skills up to date, and ensuring advice given is evidence-informed.
- [Duty of candour](duty-of-candour.md) — The professional duty to be open and honest when something goes wrong: act to put it right, explain fully and promptly, and apologise.
- [Delegation](delegation.md) — Transferring the performance of a task to another person while retaining accountability for the delegation decision: delegate only within the other person's competence, supervise adequately, and confirm the outcome meets the required standard.
- [Raising concerns](raising-concerns.md) — Acting without delay when there is a risk to patient safety or public protection, escalating through appropriate channels, and never obstructing others who raise concerns.
- [Safeguarding](safeguarding.md) — Protecting people who are vulnerable or at risk from harm, neglect or abuse, sharing information appropriately and keeping to relevant law.
- [Revalidation](revalidation.md) — The three-yearly NMC process by which registrants demonstrate continued fitness to practise: practice hours, CPD, reflective accounts, reflective discussion, feedback and confirmation.
- [Fitness to practise](fitness-to-practise.md) — A registrant's suitability to remain on the register without restriction — the NMC investigates concerns about conduct, competence or health.
- [Supernumerary status](supernumerary-status.md) — Students on NMC-approved programmes are additional to the workforce during practice placements: they learn through participation but are not counted in staffing numbers.
- [Preceptorship](preceptorship.md) — A structured period of support for newly registered professionals as they transition from student to accountable practitioner.
